rationale: Keep: the citing paper is a theoretical generalized-Nash bargaining model of vertical supply-chain contracting and explicitly says it "extends ... bargaining-in-channels literature," while the cited 2003 paper is a core bargaining theory of distribution channels with overlapping constructs (wholesale pricing, coordination, two-part tariffs, renegotiation). Given this strong conceptual/method overlap and a direct DOI match, this is not a clearly incidental mention.
